etymology.description: 首 (head/face) + 卩 (kneeling person) -> depicts a person's head and body
etymology.interpretation: Originally represented a head and its body in a kneeling posture, emphasizing “head”. The depiction was extended semantically to represent a 'page', as pages bear leading information like a head does for a body. The concept of 'head' is also reflected in its modern usage as a radical in characters related to parts of the head or face.
etymology.simplification: 页 is a cursive shorthand of the traditional 頁, simplifying the structure while maintaining the core shape of a head.

examples.chinese: 请打开书的第十页(請打開書的第十頁)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng dǎkāi shū de dì shí yè.
examples.english: Please open to page ten of the book.

examples.chinese: 这本书每一页都有插图(這本書每一頁都有插圖)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū měi yī yè dōu yǒu chātú.
examples.english: Every page of this book has illustrations.

examples.chinese: 我昨天晚上读了三十页(我昨天晚上讀了三十頁)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zuótiān wǎnshang dúle sānshí yè.
examples.english: I read thirty pages last night.
